title: Woodshed Public Progress Snapshot
description: The PUBLIC, READ-ONLY projection of Chris's running practice state (the in-app object S / mission-control.json). This is the data sibling of the page-image bundle: render_snapshot.py serializes the private 'brain' into this clean, stable contract, dropping everything private. The public Woodshed site (and the future hub embed, wood-46/47/48) loads ONLY this file to show real progress (streak, week, tunes, PRs). NOTHING here may carry private free-text, identifiers, file paths, or raw logs — see the 'omitted' note below and the per-field privacy descriptions. This file is generated; never hand-edit. Validate output against this schema with render_snapshot.py's built-in stdlib check (no jsonschema dependency).

__comment__.description: Free-text banner carried forward from the source (e.g. the illustrative-vs-live flag). Consumers MUST ignore it; it never contains progress data. Present so the example snapshot stays clearly labeled as illustrative.

schemaVersion.description: Version of THIS public contract (independent of the app and of the upstream mission-control contract). Bump when the public shape changes so the hub embed (wood-46/47/48) can guard against it. e.g. '1.0'.

generatedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp this snapshot was generated. Source: render_snapshot.py at projection time (not the upstream generatedAt, which is preserved under source.exportedAt).

source.description: Provenance of this snapshot, for transparency. Records WHICH input was projected and WHEN the upstream brain was exported. Carries no private content.

input.description: Which input the generator projected: 'mission-control.json' (live export) or 'mission-control.example.json' (illustrative fallback when no live export is on disk).

exportedAt.description: The upstream generatedAt timestamp (when the brain was exported), preserved for traceability. Null if the input lacked one.

illustrative.description: True when this snapshot was projected from the example fallback (representative shape, NOT Chris's live numbers). The public site should label it accordingly. False only when projected from a real live export.

streak.description: Current consecutive-day practice streak. Source: S.streak. PUBLIC: a single non-identifying count.

lastDay.description: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) of the last day that counted toward the streak, or null. Source: S.lastDay. PUBLIC: a date only, no log content.

week.description: The current training week (public framing). Source: dayInfo() / week math. Carries the week number and the day's curriculum focus theme only — no per-block plan text.

index.description: Zero-based week number since EPOCH (2026-01-05). Source: week.index.

focus.description: The day's curriculum focus theme, e.g. 'Jam Simulation'. Source: week.focus. PUBLIC: a short theme label, not a plan.

primaryTune.description: Tune id of this week's primary tune (the one being actively built). Source: primaryTune. Cross-references a key in 'tunes' and an id in 'rotation'.

rotation.description: Ordered list of every tune id in the rotation pool, in TUNES order. PUBLIC: ids only (titles live under 'tunes'). Source: rotation.

tunes.description: Per-tune PUBLIC progress map, keyed by tune id. Projected from S.tuneStatus / the upstream 'tunes' map. Includes ONLY the shareable progress fields below. DELIBERATELY OMITS any private journal/lore/notes free-text, per-block log free-text, and feedback content — none of which exist on this object by construction.

title.description: Display title, e.g. 'Recorda Me'. Source: tunes[id].title. PUBLIC: a song title only.

rungStatus.description: Count of memorization rungs cleared, 0-5. Source: tunes[id].rungStatus. PUBLIC: a progress count. The raw rungDates array (which carries specific calendar dates) is intentionally reduced to this count + 'verified' boolean; see 'omitted'.

memorized.description: True when the tune is fully owned (all 5 rungs + verified). Source: tunes[id].memorized.

verified.description: True when the final solo rung was verified. Source: derived from tunes[id].verifiedDate (boolean-ized — the specific verification DATE is intentionally dropped to avoid leaking a precise activity calendar). PUBLIC: a yes/no badge.

isPrimary.description: True if this tune is the current week's primary. Source: id === primaryTune. Convenience flag for the embed.

weeklyObjectives.description: This week's contract progress (the measurable goals shown on the Today page), projected to a public progress summary. Source: weeklyObjectives. Labels are short objective descriptions written by the curriculum, not personal notes.

label.description: Short human description of the objective, e.g. 'Speed: beat ANY tempo PR'. Source: weeklyObjectives[].label.

current.description: Progress so far this week. Source: weeklyObjectives[].current.

target.description: Goal value for this week. Source: weeklyObjectives[].target.

met.description: True when current >= target. Source: weeklyObjectives[].met, else derived as current >= target.

weeklyObjectivesSummary.description: Roll-up of weeklyObjectives for a compact embed badge (e.g. '2 of 4 goals met'). Source: derived from weeklyObjectives.

met.description: Count of objectives with met === true.

prTargets.description: Personal-record ladders: current best vs goal for each speed/technique metric. Source: prTargets. PUBLIC: numeric bests/goals + last-logged date. No log free-text.

best.description: Current best bpm logged (0 when never logged). Source: prTargets[id].best.

goal.description: Target bpm for this metric. Source: prTargets[id].goal.

lastLogged.description: ISO date of the most recent PR entry, or null. Source: prTargets[id].lastLogged. PUBLIC: a date only.

pctToGoal.description: Convenience: round(100 * best / goal), capped at 100; null if goal missing. Derived for the embed's progress bars.

$omitted.description: NON-SCHEMA documentation block (consumers ignore it). Records what is intentionally EXCLUDED from the public projection and WHY, so the policy is auditable in the contract itself.
fields: tunes[].rungDates  — raw 5-element array of calendar dates each rung cleared; reduced to rungStatus (count) + verified (boolean). WHY: precise per-rung dates expose a fine-grained activity calendar with no public value.
tunes[].verifiedDate — exact verification date; boolean-ized to 'verified'. WHY: same activity-calendar concern.
tunes[].pinned / pinnedTune — manual pin state. WHY: internal steering control, not progress.
reviewTunes / reviewSchedule — spaced-review internals (review lick ids, exam text). WHY: internal curriculum mechanics, not public progress.
monthlyObjectives — flagged [confirm:] upstream and partly free-text steering notes. WHY: not stable public progress; may carry steering prose.
appStateKey — localStorage key identifier. WHY: implementation/identifier detail, no public value.

any journal / lore / notes / per-block log free-text / feedback content — WHY: private prose, the core privacy line; none of it is even present on the mission-control object by construction, but called out explicitly so future upstream additions don't silently leak.
device identifiers / file paths — WHY: never public; not present upstream, called out to forbid future leakage.
